mnemonics.title: Allocation Strategies Order
mnemonics.content: Fast Birds Worry: First-Fit (Fast), Best-Fit (Birds), Worst-Fit (Worry) - Remember the order of memory allocation strategies

mnemonics.title: Fragmentation Types
mnemonics.content: Internal Inside, External Everywhere: Internal fragmentation occurs within allocated blocks, External in free spaces between blocks

mnemonics.title: Memory Partition Steps
mnemonics.content: Split, Assign, Track: 1. Split memory into partitions 2. Assign to processes 3. Track using allocation tables

mnemonics.title: Address Protection
mnemonics.content: Base Before, Limit Later: Base register stores starting address, Limit register defines maximum offset allowed

acronyms.usage: Core concept of adjacent memory block allocation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine RAM as a bookshelf - processes are books that need contiguous shelf space
Picture a parking lot with small unused spaces (external) vs cars too small for their spots (internal)
Visualize a fence marking the start of a property (base address) with 'No Trespassing' sign
Think of a chocolate bar being broken into pieces - each piece must stay intact

analogies.analogy: Like parking in the first available spot that fits your car - quick but not always optimal
Hotel rooms with fixed sizes - you get a room that's either too big or too small for your needs
Custom-tailored suits - memory allocated exactly to process requirements, but requires skilled tailoring (OS management)
Defragmenting a hard drive like organizing files to the front of a drawer to create contiguous free space
